message:
test(fcp-conformance/session-ack): pin attestation, suite-floor, and transcript-binding (br-88c9l)

The responder->initiator MeshSessionAck verify path enforces several
interlocking invariants that live inline in fcp-protocol/src/session.rs
but had no cross-crate conformance coverage. New tests pin the public
verify_ack_attested + MeshSessionAck::verify contract:

- ack_round_trip_passes_attestation_verify (happy path)
- ack_from_not_matching_hello_to_is_rejected_as_hello_mismatch
- ack_to_not_matching_hello_from_is_rejected_as_hello_mismatch
- ack_picks_a_suite_the_hello_did_not_offer_is_rejected
  (AckSuiteNotOffered)
- ack_signed_by_a_different_responder_is_rejected_as_node_mismatch
- ack_with_stale_timestamp_is_rejected_with_skew_error
- ack_with_tampered_signature_is_rejected_as_invalid_signature
- ack_without_signature_is_rejected_as_missing_signature
- ack_transcript_binds_to_hello_ephemeral_pubkey
  (an ack signed against hello_a must not verify against hello_b that
  shares everything but eph_pubkey — locks the cross-handshake binding
  that prevents ack re-attribution)
- ack_transcript_binds_to_hello_nonce (same property for the hello nonce)

All 10 tests use only the public fcp_protocol::session API so a
regression in MeshSessionAck::verify, verify_ack_suite_against_floor,
or the ack transcript layout fails conformance directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
